free_text: Note on the 250 keV resonance in the total neutron
cross section of 6Li
protons on lithium-7. Pseudo-monoenergetic
neutron source. Vernier time scaling to determine
energy scale relative to the velocity of light.
Neutrons have an energy spread of 100-200 keV centered
at about an incident energy of 250 keV.
Five right cylinder samples measured concurrently
including iron, carbon, aluminum, a thin lithium and
a thick lithium. The iron and aluminum samples were
used as additional verification of the energy scale.
Time-of-flight.
2 cm thick liquid scintillator.
Uncertainties given include contributions
from:
.uncertainty in flight path length,
.uncertainty in frequency of crystal oscillator,
.uncertainty in time-analyzer calibration,
.uncertainty in gamma-ray position,
.uncertainty in velocity of light,
.cumulative energy scale uncertainties,
.location of Li-6 resonance maximum,
.combined uncertainties associated with 6Li resonance.
Approved by A.B.Smith, 1980/5/12.
BIB correction.
BIB section updated
Cylinder of 5.538-Cm length, 3.504-Cm diameter, and
0.2563 Atom/barn thickness.
Energy scales determined in comparison with iron
resonances between 170 and 270 keV as per ENDF/B-IV.
Total cross section magnitudes verified by comparison
with carbon total cross sections at 245 keV.
Data received on punched cards, A.B. Smith,
